label: sparse_baseline

persona.name: Maya, the Solo Brand Designer
persona.summary: Maya is a freelance brand designer who works solo, serving multiple clients simultaneously. She relies on efficient digital tools to manage design assets, maintain brand consistency, and share work with clients—all while scaling her capacity without expanding her team.
goals: Create professional, on-brand design assets quickly using templates as starting points
Organize and access brand colors and guidelines without manual setup for each project
Export and deliver polished assets to clients efficiently
pains: Starting from scratch on each client project wastes time and creative energy
Ensuring consistent brand colors and guidelines across assets is tedious
Managing client feedback and revision rounds without losing track
motivations: Speed up project delivery to take on more clients and increase revenue
Build a reputation for delivering polished, consistent brand work
Provide clients with seamless, professional collaboration experiences
Scale personal capacity without hiring team members
objections: Steep learning curve or onboarding overhead for new tools
Inflexible templates that don't match their unique creative vision
Concerns about data privacy when sharing client work digitally
decision_triggers: Time spent on repetitive brand setup tasks exceeds creative work
Client requests for design revisions become unmanageable
Difficulty sharing work and gathering client feedback efficiently
Desire to take on additional projects without expanding overhead
Need to maintain consistent visual identity across multiple client projects
sample_quotes: I need to get designs to my clients faster without sacrificing quality or brand consistency.
Every new client project means starting over with their color palette and brand rules—it's exhausting.
I'd love a tool that lets me set up brand guidelines once and reuse them across projects.
Sharing design files with clients and tracking feedback is my biggest bottleneck right now.

journey_stages.mindset: Knows her current workflow is inefficient but hasn't committed to changing tools.
key_actions: Searching for solutions to brand consistency problems
content_preferences: Case studies from other freelance designers

journey_stages.mindset: Actively testing tools to see if they solve her workflow pain points without overwhelming complexity.
key_actions: Exporting sample assets to evaluate output quality
Checking how brand kit features work with her existing projects
content_preferences: Demo videos of asset export and client sharing
Tutorials on setting up brand kits and templates
Comparisons with other design management tools

journey_stages.mindset: Ready to commit if the tool proves it saves time, maintains quality, and simplifies client collaboration.
key_actions: Setting up a brand kit with colors and guidelines
Creating a white-label project for client deliverables
Requesting custom features or asking about data privacy guarantees

source_evidence.claim: Create professional, on-brand design assets quickly using templates as starting points
Organize and access brand colors and guidelines without manual setup for each project
Export and deliver polished assets to clients efficiently
Starting from scratch on each client project wastes time and creative energy
Ensuring consistent brand colors and guidelines across assets is tedious
Managing client feedback and revision rounds without losing track
Speed up project delivery to take on more clients and increase revenue
Build a reputation for delivering polished, consistent brand work
Provide clients with seamless, professional collaboration experiences
Scale personal capacity without hiring team members
Steep learning curve or onboarding overhead for new tools
Inflexible templates that don't match their unique creative vision
Concerns about data privacy when sharing client work digitally

hallucination_flags: Managing client feedback and revision rounds without losing track - not directly evidenced in source records
Specific quote about 'sharing design files with clients and tracking feedback is my biggest bottleneck' - not sourced from records
rationale: Core claims (solo freelancer, brand designer, using templates/colors/export/client sharing tools) are well-supported by behavioral events and HubSpot data. However, the specific pain around feedback management and one quoted statement lack direct source attribution; quotes appear inferred rather than directly recorded. Goals and vocabulary are grounded in observed tool usage patterns but some pains extrapolate beyond available evidence.
